title: indigastrum costatum
intro: Indigastrum costatum, also known as the 'Costate Indigowood', is a plant species belonging to the family Polygalaceae. It is a woody climber native to tropical Africa, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its ability to lower fever, treat wounds, and combat infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Indigastrum costatum can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste, and is often used in herbal remedies to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
